summary: Mechanical cross-platform pass over ~47 decomp-workflow scripts that hardcoded Windows `.exe` suffixes and Windows-only paths, so the tooling could not run on macOS/Linux. mwcceppc/mwldeppc/sjiswrap intentionally kept `.exe` because they are Windows PE binaries meant to run under wibo.
decomp_lessons: Tool binaries split into two classes: native (objdiff-cli, dtk) which must drop .exe on non-Windows, and vendored Windows PE compiler binaries (mwcceppc/mwldeppc/sjiswrap) which must KEEP .exe everywhere because they run under wibo, not natively.

Hardcoded LAN IPs/hosts (e.g. an Ollama GPU box) and hardcoded Windows Python paths should be env-var overridable (DECOMP_GPU_HOST, DECOMP_OLLAMA_PORT, DECOMP_GPU_SSH, CODEX_CMD, WINPY) so the same scripts work across contributors' machines and OSes.
smart_moves: Verified safety cheaply: every modified .py passed `python -m py_compile`, and the PR explicitly notes no functional change on Windows before merging a 47-file mechanical sweep.
Used a single `os.name == 'nt'` ternary pattern repeated across all 42 tool scripts instead of a shared helper, keeping the diff trivially reviewable per-file.

key_files.role: Central compile gate; switched OBJDIFF_CLI resolution to platform-conditional path (later extended further in PR #11).
Replaced hardcoded 10.0.0.3 Ollama host and Windows codex.cmd path with DECOMP_GPU_HOST/CODEX_CMD env overrides.
Changed default interpreter from a hardcoded Windows python.exe path to PATH python3/python, with WINPY as an override for Windows/WSL.
Converted Windows backslash path handling to pathlib for cross-platform path joins.
